name: Qurrah ibn Abi Qurrah
title: Brave of Ghaffar
shortDescription: A brave warrior from the Ghaffar tribe who went to the battlefield reciting epic poetry and was martyred courageously.
fullStory:
Qurrah ibn Abi Qurrah al-Ghaffari was a Shia and a brave man from the Ghaffar tribe (the same tribe Abu Dharr al-Ghaffari belonged to). Upon hearing the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) movement, he reached his caravan.

On Ashura, with deep love for the Ahl al-Bayt (AS), he sought permission from the Imam to fight. Qurrah entered the battlefield with exemplary courage.

He recited an impassioned poetry in the battlefield, saying: 'The Ghaffar and Khindif families know that I am among the brave, and I defend the Prophet's children with my sword...'

After killing a number of enemy soldiers, Qurrah fell to the ground with a body drenched in blood and attained the great grace of martyrdom. His name is recorded in authentic historical sources as one of the devotees of Wilayah.
quoteArabic: قَدْ عَلِمَتْ حَقّاً بَنُو غِفارِ... بِأَنَّنِي اللَّيْثُ الأَخُو الْمِغْوارِ...
quote: (His Rajaz in battle:) The Ghaffar tribes truly know that I am a brave and warrior lion...
